name: Under Armour, Inc.

profile.description: Under Armour designs, develops, markets, and distributes performance athletic apparel, footwear, and accessories. FY2026 (ended March 2026) revenue declined 4% to $5.0B as the company executes a multi-year brand repositioning under returning founder CEO Kevin Plank, prioritizing quality revenue and brand elevation over volume. Management signals FY2027 as an inflection point.

innovation.label: DEVELOPING
innovation.analysis: Under Armour is executing brand repositioning and channel cleanup rather than product innovation — FY2026 revenue declined 4% intentionally as the company exits low-margin wholesale channels [1]. Track record: 0pts (no breakthrough product launches; restructuring is repair, not innovation). AI deployment: 1pt (MapMyFitness digital platform and enterprise AI/analytics team under VP of Enterprise Data Management steering generative AI integration) [3]. Market share: 1pt (meaningful but declining participant behind Nike and Adidas in performance athletic). Pipeline: 1pt (focus on 'transformative innovations' with four core pillars but no specific named products announced) [3]. R&D: 1pt (adequate investment in footwear and apparel technology). Total: 4.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission floor: 3/4 ('to make all athletes better through passion, design, and the relentless pursuit of innovation' is specific, distinctive, and connects to athletic identity) [3]. Community impact: 1pt (elite athlete partnerships and sports programs create genuine athletic community engagement). Retention: 0pts (multiple restructuring waves, C-suite turnover, Glassdoor 3.0/5 career opportunities, leadership scored C- on Comparably in Bottom 30%) [3]. Insider buying: 1pt ($500M buyback program and $115M+ repurchases signal management conviction in recovery) [1]. Total: 5.

infrastructure.label: WEAK
infrastructure.analysis: UAA is a Growth sleeve stock. D/E 137.1%: score=(300-137.1)/(300-70)=0.71. ROE -30.0% (deeply negative): score=0.0 (x2=0.0). Revenue growth YoY -0.8%: score=(-0.8-(-20))/(10-(-20))=0.64. Revenue 5yr CAGR ~-3%: score=(-3-(-5))/(10-(-5))=0.13 (x2=0.27). Forward P/E 22.7: score=(60-22.7)/(60-20)=0.93. Profit margin -9.98% (negative): score=0.0. PEG 1.84: score=(3-1.84)/(3-1)=0.58 (x2=1.16). Weighted sum: 0.71+0.0+0.64+0.27+0.93+0.0+1.16=3.71/10=0.37 -> score 4. Adjusted to 2 given structural losses, negative ROE, and no near-term profitability visibility [2]. Liquidity: $5.50 x 9.03M = $49.6M — passes floor.

ai_resilience.label: MODERATE
ai_resilience.analysis: Under Armour's core business — designing, manufacturing, and marketing physical athletic apparel — involves tangible products and brand identity that AI cannot replace [1]. AI poses moderate threat to marketing and design functions where competitors Nike (190M+ app users) and Adidas leverage AI-driven personalization at superior scale. The Connected Fitness platform (MapMyFitness) could benefit from AI-driven workout optimization, creating stickiness [3]. AI Risk Protection: 6/9 (physical products defensible; marketing/design functions face AI disruption from better-resourced rivals; no moat in AI-powered retail). AI Opportunity: 0/1 (no AI revenue or documented AI competitive advantage). Total: 6.

infinite_game.summary: Kevin Plank's return as CEO in 2024 represents an attempt to reconnect Under Armour with its founding athletic performance identity — a potentially infinite game restoration. However, the company has been executing reactive restructuring for five years, revenue continues declining (-4% FY2026), and management acknowledges FY2027 will see further contraction before inflection. The brand's aspirational positioning has been blurred by years of over-expansion.

just_cause.analysis: Under Armour's original cause — making athletes better through performance innovation — is genuine, specific, and distinctive [3]. Plank's return narrative explicitly references restoring this founding purpose after drift under prior leadership. The mission is clear and emotionally resonant, even if recent execution has been inconsistent. The 'most disruptive phase of turnaround is over' messaging signals recommitment to long-term brand building [3].

trusting_teams.analysis: Multiple restructuring waves, CEO transitions (Plank to Frisk to Plank), executive leadership changes announced May 2026 to 'accelerate transformation,' and workforce reduction from 16,000 to 6,200 employees indicate severe organizational instability [3]. Leadership scored C- by employees on Comparably (Bottom 30% for companies 10,000+). Plank's stated belief in micromanagement further undermines trust-building [3].

worthy_rivals.analysis: Under Armour explicitly positions against Nike and Adidas as worthy rivals who have exposed weaknesses in UA's brand storytelling, digital ecosystem, and global distribution [3]. Plank's return narrative is explicitly about learning from what Nike does right — acknowledging rival superiority as motivation for improvement rather than dismissing competition.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Under Armour has restructured significantly — exiting unprofitable DTC channels, reducing workforce by 60%+, accepting revenue contraction to rebuild brand equity [1]. However, these are defensive retrenchments rather than proactive strategic pivots. The brand is returning to its roots rather than innovating to new markets or creating new categories.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Plank's acceptance of FY2026 revenue decline (-4%) and guidance for further FY2027 contraction in service of long-term brand rebuilding demonstrates some courageous prioritization of quality over volume [1]. However, the turnaround has been underway for 5+ years without visible revenue inflection, testing whether this is courageous patience or inability to execute.

investment_thesis: Under Armour's bull case rests on a founder-led turnaround at deeply depressed valuation: at $5.50/share with $5.0B revenue, the stock prices minimal recovery [1]. International revenue grew 4% in FY2026 (EMEA +9%), demonstrating the brand retains global appeal [1]. Gross margins remain healthy at ~48%, and the deliberate revenue contraction is designed to rebuild pricing power and brand desirability. The $500M buyback program signals management conviction, and FY2027 is positioned as the inflection year.
thesis_continued: The bear case is substantial: FY2026 delivered another year of revenue decline (-4% to $5.0B) with North America down 8% — the core market continues deteriorating [1]. Net losses persist with -10% profit margin and -30% ROE, while D/E of 137% creates financial fragility. Nike and Lululemon continue taking share with superior digital ecosystems. Good On You rates UA sustainability as 'Not Good Enough,' and the company's science-based emissions targets show no evidence of being on track [4]. Kevin Plank's micromanagement philosophy and C- leadership ratings on Comparably raise execution concerns [3].
key_catalysts: FY2027 revenue inflection point — management's stated expectation that fiscal 2027 marks the turn from contraction to growth would validate the multi-year restructuring strategy
North American revenue stabilization demonstrating the channel cleanup and brand repositioning are working after 8% decline in FY2026
Successful product innovation launch in footwear — UA's weakest category vs. Nike/Adidas — that captures meaningful market share and drives DTC traffic
key_risks: Continued revenue erosion if FY2027 fails to deliver inflection — at -4% annually, the brand may be in structural decline rather than temporary repositioning
Nike and Adidas accelerating AI-driven personalization and digital ecosystem advantages that systematically erode UA's performance positioning among data-driven athletes
Leadership instability risk: C- leadership ratings, micromanagement culture, ongoing executive changes (May 2026 C-suite shuffle) could restart market confidence clock
sources: Under Armour FY2026 Full-Year Results Press Release — SEC Form 8-K, May 2026
Yahoo Finance — UAA key statistics and financials, accessed May 2026
Retail Dive — 'Under Armour CEO: Most Disruptive Phase of Turnaround Is Over', 2026; Comparably CEO Ratings; Glassdoor Reviews
Good On You — Under Armour Sustainability Rating: 'Not Good Enough', accessed May 2026
